goal: Fix #83: session-only evidenceRead uses read-range --query to find real anchor

summary.title: Session-only evidenceRead uses read-range --query to find real anchor
summary.what: Change buildEvidenceReadAction to return read-range --query <query> for session-only hits (matchSeq===null) when the query is available, instead of read-page offset=0. Update resolveAnchorSeq to fall back to seq=0 when query finds no message hits (instead of throwing). Update acceptance gate fixture and desiredContextMode to expect read-range for session-only hits. Add query field to DogfoodExpectedContext.
summary.why: Session-only hits returned read-page offset=0, which for long sessions (86-message pi, 66-message codex) read irrelevant session beginnings (greetings) instead of relevant evidence. 6/6 dogfood agents confirmed this defect on real data. The fix lets resolveAnchorSeq use searchTopHitInSession to locate the real evidence anchor inside the session transcript.
summary.user_goal: Fix #83: session-only evidenceRead uses read-range --query to find real anchor

decisions.point: How to find the real anchor for session-only hits
decisions.chose: Reuse existing resolveAnchorSeq → searchTopHitInSession path via read-range --query, no new DB scan code
decisions.rationale: resolveAnchorSeq already had the logic to find anchors by query (used by read-range --query CLI option). Reusing it avoids duplicating the FTS + rerankHits logic and keeps the codebase DRY.
rejected: Write a new lightweight message scan in buildEvidenceReadAction — rejected because it would require DB access in a pure function and duplicate existing search logic

decisions.point: What to do when query finds no message hits in the session
decisions.chose: Fall back to seq=0 instead of throwing
decisions.rationale: Session-level hits often come from compact/summary text where the query terms appear as concepts but not verbatim in raw messages. Throwing would make the evidenceRead command fail; falling back to seq=0 is no worse than the old read-page offset=0 behavior and still returns a usable bounded window.
rejected: Throw an error when query finds no hits — rejected because it would break evidenceRead for compact/summary hits, which are the majority of session-only hits

decisions.point: Whether to add query to DogfoodExpectedContext type
decisions.chose: Add optional query field to DogfoodExpectedContext
decisions.rationale: The acceptance gate uses context.query ?? item.query to determine the query for read-range. Adding it to the type makes the contract explicit and allows per-fixture query overrides.

architectural_claims: Session-only hits now use read-range --query instead of read-page offset=0
resolveAnchorSeq falls back to seq=0 when query finds no hits instead of throwing
behavioral_changes: find --json evidenceRead for session-only hits now returns read-range with --query instead of read-page with --offset 0
read-range --query no longer throws when no message hits are found; it returns messages starting from seq=0
